title: cynodon indicus
intro: Cynodon indicus, also known as Dallis grass, is a species of grass native to tropical and subtropical regions of Africa and Asia. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, such as fever, sore throats, and digestive problems, and is also believed to have antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. In herbal preparations, Cynodon indicus is often made into teas, decoctions, or infusions, or used in combination with other herbs to create tonics and remedies for various health conditions.
